layout_description:
Option: OPT-1
Slide description: SINGLE-CONTAINER FORMAL TABLE LAYOUT. The slide uses a top-to-bottom orientation with the title at the top and a single full-width table container filling the remaining vertical space. The table is a formal regulatory/legal data structure presenting tax categories, rates, applicable activities, and legal basis in a four-column grid. The design follows the Flat Geometry Table pattern (Section 4 Option C) with no filled header bars, accent tabs beneath column headers, light grey row label cells, and thin dashed grey separator lines between rows. The layout maximizes vertical space utilization while maintaining generous padding appropriate for the sparse content density (4 data rows). The table spans approximately 85% of the slide width, horizontally centered, with equal margins on left and right. Vertical distribution is balanced: title occupies the top 12% of slide height, with a visible gap below it, and the table container fills the remaining space down to a comfortable bottom margin. All column edges align to an invisible 4-column grid, and row separators are positioned at consistent intervals to create visual rhythm without consuming excessive vertical space.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: top-to-bottom
  Regions: 2
  Allocation: [0.12, 0.88]

Containers:
  C0 (FULL-WIDTH FORMAL TAX TABLE): This container holds a four-column regulatory table presenting tax categories for virtual asset activities. The table uses the Flat Geometry Table design pattern (Section 4 Option C) — the most appropriate choice for formal/regulatory/legal content. STRUCTURE: Four equal-width columns arranged left to right: Tax Type | Rate | Applicable Activity | Legal Basis. Each column spans 25% of the table width with no gaps between columns (columns are visually separated by alignment and content, not by physical gutters). COLUMN HEADERS: Plain bold text in heading font, left-aligned within each column, positioned at the top of the table. No filled header bar. Below each header label sits a small accent tab (brand primary color, very thin in height — approximately 3% of table height, narrow width — approximately 15% of column width, top corners slightly rounded, bottom edge square, left-aligned directly beneath the header text, sitting fully below the text with no overlap). Immediately below and touching each accent tab is a thin solid horizontal line in brand dark color, spanning that column's full width. The four header lines sit at the same vertical position but do not connect across columns — each is confined to its column width with small white gutters between columns. DATA ROWS: Four data rows distributed evenly across the remaining vertical space using equal flexGrow values. Each row occupies approximately 20% of the table height. Row 1: Income Tax | 10-35% | Mining, staking, airdrops, crypto payments | Section 3, Income Tax Act. Row 2: Capital Gains | 15% | Gains on disposal of virtual assets | Capital Gains Tax Act. Row 3: Excise Duty | 10% | Fees charged by exchanges & service providers | Finance Act 2025. Row 4: Withholding Tax | Various | Payments to non-residents for crypto services | Income Tax Act. FIRST COLUMN (Tax Type) TREATMENT: Each cell in the first column is a light grey filled rectangle (no border, no rounded corners, spanning the full column width and most of the row height — approximately 85% of row height, vertically centered within the row band) with bold text in brand dark color, left-aligned with a small left inset (approximately 3% of column width). The grey fill visually distinguishes the row labels from the data columns. OTHER COLUMNS (Rate, Applicable Activity, Legal Basis): Plain body text in body font, brand text color, left-aligned with a small left inset, vertically centered within the row band. No background fill — white/transparent. Text wraps naturally within the column width; font size is reduced slightly if necessary to fit multi-line content comfortably within the row height (target: medium body font size, reduced to fit 20-word cells if needed). ROW SEPARATORS: Thin dashed grey horizontal lines between consecutive rows (between Row 1 and Row 2, between Row 2 and Row 3, between Row 3 and Row 4). No separator after Row 4. Each separator spans the full table width from the left edge of the first column to the right edge of the fourth column. Separators are positioned at the exact boundary between row bands — they sit tight to the content with minimal space above and below, consistent with the dense content spacing principle (Section 0.1). VISUAL HIERARCHY: The first column's grey fill and bold text create clear visual weight, establishing the tax type as the primary identifier for each row. The Rate column uses bold text (since rates are key data points) while the other two columns use regular weight. The accent tabs beneath column headers add a jewel-like detail that signals 'this is a formal, carefully designed table' without adding visual clutter. ALIGNMENT: All text in each column shares the same left edge (left-aligned with consistent inset). All row bands across all four columns share identical top and bottom edges — this is a hard constraint from the row-group detection rule (Step 0.5c). The four row separators are perfectly horizontal and aligned across all columns. SPACING: The table has generous internal padding on all sides (approximately 4% of slide width on left/right, 3% of slide height on top/bottom) to prevent content from crowding the container edges. This padding is appropriate for the sparse content density — four rows with moderate text length. CONTAINER BACKGROUND: White fill. No border (the table's internal structure provides sufficient visual definition). No rounded corners (flat geometry aesthetic). No shadow or glow (formal tables do not use decorative effects). The container fills the available vertical space between the title and the bottom margin, maximizing slide coverage while maintaining comfortable whitespace at the edges.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BACKGROUND LAYER): INACTIVE. The slide content (single table) fills the available space sufficiently. The table container itself provides all necessary visual structure through its internal column headers, accent tabs, row separators, and grey-filled label cells. Adding a bottom decorative layer would serve no purpose — it would not group multiple containers (there is only one container), and it would not fill empty space (the table already fills the slide). Per Rule D1, the bottom decorative layer is used ONLY when content does not fill the slide or when multiple containers need visual grouping. Neither condition applies here. The slide is well-filled by the table container alone.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER): INACTIVE. This slide contains a single table with no cross-container relationships, no process flow arrows, and no elements that span multiple containers. There are no connectors, annotation lines, or overlay elements required. Per Rule E3 (top-layer minimalism), the top layer is used only for elements that genuinely span containers or sit above all containers. Since this slide has only one container and no inter-container relationships, the top layer remains empty.
